messages.content: You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
Several of my cattle are drooling, lame, and have blisters in the mouth and on the feet, and they've gone off feed. What is it?

Drooling with blisters in the mouth and on the feet, lameness and a sudden drop in feeding across several animals strongly suggests foot-and-mouth disease (FMD), a highly contagious viral disease of cattle and other cloven-hoofed animals. It spreads very fast between animals and across farms through contact, movement and shared equipment. It is rarely fatal in adults but causes serious weight and milk loss, and it is a reportable disease, so isolate affected animals at once, stop all movement of stock on and off your farm, and contact an animal-health officer or veterinary authority promptly. Provide soft feed and clean water and let the sores heal, and disinfect thoroughly. Prevention is vaccination where FMD is common, strict quarantine of new animals, and biosecurity.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
My cattle are getting thin and weak with pale gums despite eating, especially near the river and bush. What could be wrong?

Progressive weight loss, weakness, pale gums (anaemia) and poor condition despite feeding, in animals grazing near rivers and bush, point toward trypanosomiasis, spread by the bite of the tsetse fly. The parasite lives in the blood and steadily drains the animal, and untreated cases waste away. Because it overlaps with heavy worm burdens and other blood diseases, this needs veterinary diagnosis, ideally a blood check, and treatment must be prescribed and given by an animal-health professional rather than guessed. Reduce the challenge by avoiding heavy tsetse areas at peak times, using fly control and traps where available, and keeping animals in good condition. Ask your vet about trypanocidal options and a worm check, since the two often go together.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
A young, well-fed cow suddenly went lame with a swollen, crackling hind leg and died within a day. What killed it?

Sudden lameness with a hot, swollen muscle that crackles under the skin from gas, high fever and rapid death in a young, thriving animal is the classic picture of blackleg, a bacterial disease from spores in the soil that usually strikes well-conditioned young cattle. It moves fast and is often fatal before you can treat. Separate the remaining stock from the suspect area and dispose of the carcass properly by deep burial or burning, because the spores contaminate the soil for a long time, so do not open the carcass in the field. This needs veterinary confirmation, and the key is prevention: vaccinate young cattle in blackleg-prone areas on a schedule your vet advises. Report sudden unexplained deaths so the cause can be confirmed.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
A cow died suddenly with dark blood coming from the nose and back end, and it isn't stiffening normally. Can I slaughter it for meat to avoid waste?

No - do not slaughter, open, skin or eat that animal, and keep people and other animals away from it. Sudden death with dark, unclotted blood from the body openings and a carcass that does not stiffen normally is a strong warning sign of anthrax, a serious bacterial disease that is dangerous to humans as well as animals and is notifiable. Opening the carcass releases spores that contaminate the soil for years and can infect you. Cover the carcass, restrict access, and contact an animal-health officer or veterinary authority immediately so they can confirm and guide safe disposal, usually by burning or deep burial under supervision. Do not move or sell any part of it. Prevention in anthrax areas is vaccination on veterinary advice. When in doubt about a sudden death, never butcher - report it.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
My goats, especially the kids, have crusty scabby sores on their lips and around the mouth. What is it?

Crusty, scabby sores on the lips, muzzle and around the mouth, most obvious in kids, are typical of orf (contagious ecthyma), a viral skin disease of goats and sheep. It usually clears on its own in a few weeks, but the sores can make young animals reluctant to suckle or feed, so they lose condition. Keep affected animals eating with soft feed, keep the sores clean, and isolate affected stock because it spreads by contact. Important: orf can spread to people through skin contact, so handle affected animals with gloves and wash your hands well. It is rarely serious, but confirm with an animal-health officer if sores are severe or do not heal, and do not confuse it with more dangerous diseases that also cause mouth lesions.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
Some of my goats are limping and their hooves smell bad and look rotten between the claws. What is wrong?

Limping with a foul smell and softened, rotting tissue between the claws is foot rot, a bacterial infection that thrives in wet, muddy, dirty conditions and spreads between animals. Move affected animals onto dry, clean ground, carefully trim away the loose, rotten hoof horn to expose and clean the area, and use a foot bath or treatment as your animal-health officer advises. Isolate badly affected animals so they do not infect the rest. Prevent it by keeping pens and walkways dry and clean, avoiding muddy standing areas, trimming hooves regularly, and quarantining new animals. Chronic or severe cases should be checked by a vet.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
My goats are coughing, breathing fast with nasal discharge, and a couple have a fever and won't eat. What could it be?

Coughing, fast or laboured breathing, nasal discharge, fever and going off feed point to pneumonia, a common and serious problem in goats and sheep, often triggered by cold, damp, draughty or dusty housing, stress, or following another infection. It can move fast in young animals. Isolate sick animals, move them to clean, dry, well-ventilated but draught-free shelter, and keep them warm with good feed and clean water. Because bacterial pneumonia needs the right antibiotic at the right dose, this is a case for an animal-health officer rather than self-treatment, and early treatment saves animals. Prevent it by avoiding overcrowding, damp and sudden chilling, improving ventilation without draughts, and reducing stress and dust. Some respiratory disease here is contagious, so confirm the cause with a vet.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
My goat's left side is suddenly swollen up like a balloon and it's uncomfortable and straining. What is happening?

A suddenly swollen, drum-tight left side with discomfort, drooling and straining is bloat, a dangerous build-up of gas in the rumen that can kill quickly if the gas cannot escape, often after eating a lot of lush legume forage, wet grass or grain. Act fast: stop feeding, get the animal up and moving and keep its head raised, which can help it belch the gas, and gentle massage of the left side may help. This is an emergency that can suffocate the animal, so contact an animal-health officer urgently, because severe cases need a vet to relieve the pressure. Prevent it by introducing rich or lush feeds gradually, not turning hungry animals onto lush legume pasture, and giving roughage before rich feed. If an animal goes down or struggles to breathe, get help immediately.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
My older birds are dying suddenly, some with swollen dark wattles and greenish droppings, and it hits the bigger birds. What is it?

Sudden deaths in older, often larger birds, with swollen, darkened wattles and combs, greenish diarrhoea and sometimes lameness, suggest fowl cholera, a bacterial disease (Pasteurella) that tends to strike adult birds and can cause heavy sudden losses. It spreads through sick and carrier birds, contaminated feed and water, and rodents or wild birds. Isolate sick birds, remove dead ones promptly and dispose of them properly, and clean and disinfect feeders, drinkers and housing. Because it is bacterial it can respond to treatment, but the right antibiotic and the decision about the flock should be guided by a vet, ideally with laboratory confirmation. Prevent it with good hygiene, rodent and wild-bird control, not mixing in birds of unknown status, and vaccination where it recurs.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
Yaya zan magance tsutsotsi (worms) a cikin kaji na gida?

Tsutsotsi na cikin kaji suna rage girma, rage ƙwai da kuma raunana kaji, musamman kajin da ke yawo a ƙasa. Alamomi sun haɗa da rama duk da ci, gashi mara kyau, da wani lokaci tsutsa a cikin kashi. Ka kiyaye tsabta: cire datti da kashi akai-akai, ka guji cunkoson kaji a wuri ɗaya, kuma ka ba su ruwa mai tsabta a cikin kwano da ba ya gurɓata da kashi. Ka yi maganin tsutsa (deworming) bisa shawarar likitan dabbobi a lokutan da ya dace maimakon kullum, domin yawan amfani na haifar da juriya. Kar ka yi amfani da magani ba tare da shawara ba, kuma ka inganta abinci domin kaji masu ƙarfi suna jure tsutsa da kyau. Idan matsalar ta yi yawa, ka tuntuɓi jami'in lafiyar dabbobi.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
How do I keep my layers producing eggs well?

Steady egg production comes from steady feeding, light, water and comfort. Feed a balanced layer ration with enough energy, protein and especially calcium for shells, and never let feed or clean water run short, because even a brief shortage drops production for days. Layers need enough total light each day (around 14 to 16 hours), so where natural daylight is short, supplement with a little evening light on a consistent schedule. Keep the house clean, well ventilated and not overcrowded, provide enough nest boxes so hens lay in them rather than on the floor, and collect eggs often to keep them clean and reduce breakage and egg-eating. Reduce stress from heat, predators and disturbance, keep a vaccination and parasite plan, and remember production naturally declines with age and during moult.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
How do I manage breeding in my goats to get healthy, regular kids?

Good kid crops come from healthy, well-grown breeding stock and the right timing. Breed does that are mature and in good body condition, not too thin or over-fat, and use a healthy, unrelated buck of good type to avoid inbreeding, replacing or rotating the buck so he does not mate his own daughters. Time matings so kidding falls when feed and weather are favourable, for instance so the doe is feeding kids when grazing is good. Flush does with slightly better feed before mating to improve conception, and make sure both does and buck are dewormed and healthy. Keep records of matings so you know due dates and can prepare clean, dry kidding shelter. Avoid breeding does too young or too often, and cull animals that repeatedly have problems.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
What makes good housing for a small poultry flock?

Good poultry housing keeps birds dry, secure, well ventilated and easy to clean. Site it on well-drained ground and build so rain does not blow in and the floor stays dry, using deep, dry litter that you keep clean. Ventilation is key: birds need fresh air to remove heat, moisture and ammonia, but without cold draughts blowing directly on them. Protect against predators and theft with secure walls, wire and a closeable door, and keep wild birds and rodents out of feed. Give enough space so birds are not overcrowded, since crowding spreads disease and pecking, and provide enough feeders, drinkers, perches and nest boxes for the number of birds. Position it where you can clean and disinfect easily between batches.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
How should I shelter my few cattle, especially in the rains and harmattan?

Cattle mainly need shade, a dry place to lie, and protection from the worst weather rather than a closed building. Provide a simple shelter or shade that keeps off the hottest sun and heaviest rain, with good airflow so it does not get hot and damp. Give them a clean, dry area to rest, because mud and dung lying areas cause foot rot and disease, so keep it drained and add bedding where you can. In the harmattan, protect young and weak animals from cold wind and dust, which can trigger pneumonia, by giving a wind-sheltered, draught-free spot. Keep feeding and watering points clean and separate from the lying area, and clean out dung regularly. Site the shelter on free-draining ground away from where water collects.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
Yaya zan kula da ɗan rago (lamb) sabon haihuwa?

Kwanaki na farko su ne mafi muhimmanci ga ɗan rago. Ka tabbatar ya sha nonon farko (colostrum) na uwa cikin sa'o'i kaɗan bayan haihuwa, domin yana ba shi garkuwar jiki da ƙarfi. Ka tsabtace ka bushe wurin haihuwa, ka shafa wa cibiya (navel) maganin gargaɗi kamar iodine domin kar cuta ta shiga. Ka kiyaye ɗan rago a wuri mai dumi, bushe kuma marar iska mai sanyi, musamman a lokacin harmattan. Ka lura yana shan nono da kyau kuma yana ƙara ƙarfi; idan uwa ba ta da nono ko ta ƙi shi, sai ka taimaka da shayarwa. Ka kula da alamun rashin lafiya kamar gudawa ko rauni, ka tuntuɓi jami'in lafiyar dabbobi idan ya cancanta. Yayin da yake girma, ka yi rigakafi da maganin tsutsa bisa shawarar likita.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
I want to start keeping rabbits. What are the basics of housing and feeding them?

Rabbits are a good small livestock for limited space if you keep them cool, clean and well fed. House them in raised hutches with good airflow and shade, because rabbits suffer badly in heat, so keep them out of direct hot sun. Keep the hutches dry and clean to prevent disease and foot problems, give each rabbit enough space, and separate bucks and manage breeding so they do not over-multiply or fight. Feed plenty of good forage and greens (grasses, legumes, vegetable trimmings) plus some concentrate or grain, introduce new feeds gradually, and avoid wet, spoiled or mouldy material. Always provide clean water, which many beginners forget. Start with a few healthy animals, keep records, and learn breeding and disease signs as you grow.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
How should I feed my catfish so they grow well without wasting feed?

Feed is your biggest cost in catfish, so feeding well protects both growth and your money. Use a good-quality floating feed where you can, because floating pellets let you watch the fish eat and judge the right amount. Feed measured amounts the fish finish within a few minutes, once or twice a day, rather than dumping in feed, since uneaten feed rots, fouls the water and wastes money. Adjust the amount as the fish grow and with the weather, because fish eat less when it is cold or the water is poor. Sort fish by size periodically, because big catfish bully and eat smaller ones, so uneven sizes mean uneven feeding. Watch the water: if it fouls or the fish stop feeding, cut back and change water before adding more feed.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
In a bad dry season, how do I keep my animals watered when sources are drying up?

When water gets scarce, manage both the supply and the animals' demand. Protect and prioritise your reliable sources, and store water where you can in clean containers or a lined pond for the worst weeks. Water animals in the cooler parts of the day and provide shade, because heat sharply raises how much they need and lose. Reduce demand by not driving animals long distances in the heat and by feeding in the cool, and consider reducing stock numbers before a severe dry season rather than watching animals weaken. Keep water points clean to avoid disease spreading where animals crowd. Pregnant, young and milking animals need water most, so serve them first, and plan ahead during the rains by harvesting and storing water for the dry months.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
Strong windstorms before the rains keep flattening my maize and sorghum. How can I reduce the damage?

Tall cereals like maize and sorghum lodge (fall over) in strong winds, especially when heavy with grain or grown soft. Reduce the risk by not over-applying nitrogen, which makes tall, weak stems, and by choosing shorter, sturdier or lodging-tolerant varieties where available. Earthing up soil around the base of the plants gives better anchorage, and proper spacing, not too dense, makes stronger individual stems. Windbreaks such as rows of trees or shrubs around the field reduce wind force over time. If plants lodge early they may partly recover, but those flattened near harvest should be cut promptly to avoid grain loss and mould. Plan field layout and variety choice before the windy period rather than after.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
During the harmattan my chicks and young animals seem weak and some are dying. Is the cold to blame?

Yes - the harmattan's cold, dry, dusty wind stresses young stock and can kill them directly through chilling and indirectly by triggering pneumonia and other infections. Chicks that are too cold huddle, stop feeding and weaken, and young animals exposed to cold draughts and dust easily get respiratory disease. Protect them with extra warmth and dry, draught-free shelter: keep brooders warmer during cold nights, block cold wind from housing without sealing off all ventilation, and provide dry bedding. Reduce dust, keep them out of the wind, and make sure they keep feeding and drinking to maintain body heat. Watch for coughing and laboured breathing and act early, calling an animal-health officer for sick animals. The very young and the weak are most at risk, so give them priority.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
Should I plant all my land at once or stagger planting across a few weeks?

Staggering planting across a couple of weeks can spread both risk and labour, but it must fit the season. The advantage is that if one planting is hit by a dry spell, pest peak or bad timing, the others may escape, and it spreads your weeding and harvest labour so peaks do not all land at once. The limit is season length: in a short or late season you cannot delay much without late plantings failing to mature, so stagger only within the safe early window and use early-maturing varieties for the later plantings. Plant first into the most reliable, moisture-retaining land once the rains are stable, and avoid pushing the last planting too late. For a short season, it is often better to plant promptly across the board with an early variety than to stagger widely.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
How do I store my harvested yams so they don't rot before I sell or eat them?

Yams store best when they are sound, cured and kept cool and airy. Harvest carefully to avoid bruising and cuts, because damaged tubers rot first, and use up or discard any that are cut or diseased rather than storing them. Cure them for a week or two in a shaded, airy place so small wounds heal and the skin toughens. Store in a well-ventilated yam barn or on racks in a shaded, dry, airy structure, ideally arranged so air moves around each tuber and you can inspect them, not piled in a heap. Check regularly and remove any that start to rot or sprout heavily, so one bad tuber does not spoil others. Keep them out of sun, rain and waterlogged ground, and protect from rodents.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
Yaya zan adana albasa bayan girbi domin kar ta ruɓe?

Sirrin adana albasa shi ne girbi da kyau da kuma bushewa (curing) yadda ya kamata. Girbe albasa lokacin da yawancin ganye suka faɗi suka bushe, sannan ka bar ta ta bushe a inuwa mai iska kusan mako biyu zuwa uku har sai bawon waje ya bushe ya yi ƙara. Ka zaɓi kawai albasa masu ƙarfi, marasa rauni ko ruɓi don adanawa - masu laushi ko rauni sai a yi amfani da su da wuri. Ka adana a wuri mai iska sosai kamar raga ko crib da aka rataya, kar ka tara ta da yawa a juna, ka guji danshi da ruwa. Ka duba akai-akai ka cire duk wadda ta fara ruɓewa. Albasar da aka adana da kyau tana iya dadewa watanni, don haka za ka iya jira ka sayar lokacin da farashi ya yi sama.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
How should I prepare my land before planting, and is heavy tillage always best?

Good land preparation gives a clean, workable seedbed without wasting your soil. Clear weeds and crop residue (but consider leaving some as mulch or working it in to feed the soil), and loosen the soil enough for roots and water to penetrate and for easy planting. Heavy, repeated tillage is not always best: it can break down soil structure, dry it out and worsen erosion on sloping land, so do enough to make a good seedbed but avoid over-cultivating. On erosion-prone or sloping land, work across the slope and keep some cover to protect the soil. Time your preparation so the soil is moist enough to work but not muddy, and finish in time to plant promptly when the rains settle. Where it suits your land, minimum tillage with mulch conserves moisture and soil.

You are FarmHealth AI, an offline agricultural and livestock-health advisor for farmers and extension officers in northern Nigeria. Give practical, region-appropriate advice. Answer in the language the user used.
When is the most important time to weed my crops, and how do I keep on top of it?

Weeds rob crops of water, nutrients and light, and the damage is greatest early, so the most important time to weed is the first few weeks after the crop emerges, the 'critical period' (often roughly the first three to six weeks depending on the crop). Keep the field clean through that window and the crop gets ahead of the weeds; let weeds dominate then and you lose yield you cannot recover. Weed early and before weeds set seed, which reduces next season's weed bank, and time weeding with any fertilizer so you feed the crop, not the weeds. Use a mix of methods: timely hand or hoe weeding, good spacing and a vigorous stand that shades out weeds, and mulching where practical. If you use a herbicide, choose the right one for your crop and weeds and follow the label exactly. After the critical period the canopy helps suppress later weeds.
